_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**1. Documentation version control system**
The workspace must maintain a controlled library of Scotland supplemental certificate templates, organised by species (cattle; sheep and goats) and movement purpose (direct slaughter; breeding and production — pre-June; breeding and production — post-June). Templates are Word documents with fillable fields; completion online before printing is required. A version switch is mandated on 1 June 2026 for breeding and production certificates. Gap from baseline: most freight forwarder document management systems are not designed to track animal health certificate versions; a simple version-controlled folder structure with date-activated switching logic (manual or automated) should be implemented immediately.

**2. Consignment pre-clearance checklist**
For every Scotland-bound cattle, sheep, or goat consignment the workspace must verify before authorising loading: (a) standard GB certificate issued and number captured; (b) correct supplemental certificate version selected and completed; (c) certificate numbers cross-referenced; (d) for slaughter — destination abattoir designation confirmed; (e) premises disease history (no bluetongue confirmed in preceding 6 months); (f) from 1 June 2026 — vaccination records or PCR test results (sample date within 7 days) obtained; (g) from 1 June 2026 — insecticide pour-on treatment record obtained (where applicable). This checklist should be embedded in the workspace's consignment management system as a mandatory gate before booking confirmation.

**3. Haulier briefing and transport compliance**
All haulage partners used for Scotland-bound livestock must receive written instructions specifying: animals must not be unloaded between origin and destination except at motorway service stations, official lay-bys on trunk/main roads, or lorry parks or similar places. Animals transiting Scotland (rather than destined for Scotland) must comply with the same transit stop restriction. This briefing must be issued per consignment, not just at onboarding. Gap from baseline: standard haulier briefings typically address welfare regulations but not bluetongue-specific transit restrictions.

**4. Supplier and consignor onboarding for June 2026 season changes**
The workspace must proactively communicate to all livestock consignors the upcoming 1 June 2026 certificate version change and the new high-risk season controls (PCR testing or vaccination requirements, insecticide pour-on obligation, general licence EXD645 pro forma). Consignors must be aware that the EXD645 pro forma is their responsibility to complete, and that the workspace will require supporting evidence (test results, vaccination records) before accepting a booking. Lead time for APHA specific licence applications is recommended at 10 days minimum; the workspace must build this into booking cut-off timelines.

**5. Monitoring and update trigger**
The workspace must assign responsibility for monitoring DAERA's bluetongue publications page and gov.scot's bluetongue movement restrictions pages, as requirements have changed multiple times in 2025–2026 and further changes are possible if environmental conditions deviate or the disease situation in Scotland changes. A monthly review cadence is the minimum; during June–September 2026 a weekly review is appropriate.

*Source: Moving cattle, sheep and goats to Scotland while Scotland is Bluetongue Free, DAERA, 2026. https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/publications/moving-cattle-sheep-and-goats-scotland-while-scotland-bluetongue-free; Movement controls from 1 June 2026, Scottish Government, March 2026. https://www.gov.scot/publications/bluetongue/pages/movement-restrictions-from-1-june-2026/*
